name: Biometric Gate / Passkeys
definition_origin: Biometric authentication on mobile started with Apple Touch ID (iPhone 5s, 2013) and Face ID (iPhone X, 2017); Android Fingerprint API (4.6+, 2015) and BiometricPrompt API (Android 9 Pie, 2018). Passkeys are the FIDO Alliance + W3C WebAuthn standard for passwordless cross-platform authentication, mainstream-launched at WWDC 2022 (Apple) and Google I/O 2023 (Google Credential Manager). Passkeys are public-key cryptographic credentials stored in iCloud Keychain / Google Password Manager / 1Password / Dashlane that authenticate via biometric (Face ID, Touch ID, fingerprint, PIN) on the user's device. Codified across: Apple ASAuthorizationController + AuthenticationServices framework (iOS 16+); Android Credential Manager API (API 34+, 2023); WebAuthn Level 3 spec (2023); FIDO2 conformance test suites; Apple HIG Authentication; Google Password Manager passkey docs; 1Password / Dashlane / Bitwarden passkey support 2023-2024. By 2025 over 5B passkey accounts created across Google + Apple + Microsoft. Distinct from magic link (item 60 — first-auth method): biometric gate / passkeys are the REPEAT-SESSION lock AND the higher-security primary auth.
core_mechanism: Three reinforcing mechanisms: (1) BIOMETRIC INHERENCE — Face ID / Touch ID / fingerprint use the user's biological factor (something they ARE), which combined with device possession (something they HAVE) provides MFA without explicit password. (2) PUBLIC-KEY CRYPTOGRAPHY — passkeys (WebAuthn) replace shared-secret passwords with asymmetric crypto: private key stored in device Secure Enclave / TEE / hardware key, public key stored on server. Auth challenge signed by private key. Phishing-resistant by design (passkey scoped to specific origin). (3) UX FRICTION ELIMINATION — repeat-login becomes 0.5-2 seconds (Face ID glance, fingerprint touch) vs 30-60 seconds for magic-link round-trip. Best for: high-frequency apps (banking, fintech), security-sensitive verticals (health, finance, enterprise), repeat-session lock for any app. Pair with magic link (item 60) for first-auth (no passkey enrolled yet) → biometric gate / passkey for repeat sessions. COPPA-friendly because no password to forget for under-13 users in compliant family apps.
neuroscience_correlate: Working memory load (Sweller cognitive load theory, item 2) zero — no recall required. Familiar somatosensory loops engage in fingerprint touch (somatosensory cortex) and facial-recognition cortex (fusiform face area for Face ID) — automated, sub-conscious. Trust calibration via security cues — biometric prompt visually communicates 'serious security'.
empirical_evidence: FIDO Alliance 2024: 5B+ passkey-enabled accounts across Google + Apple + Microsoft. Google 2024 internal data: passkey users sign in 4× faster than password users + 0% credential phishing rate. Apple Face ID success rate: 99%+ in good lighting; Touch ID: 95%+. Apple WWDC 2024 passkey adoption: 2.7M iCloud Keychain passkeys generated in 2024 alone. Stytch 2024 passkey case study: enterprise SaaS clients seeing 40-60% passkey adoption among existing user base within 6 months of offering. 1Password 2024: 86% of users who try passkeys continue using them. Google Password Manager 2024: 15M+ passkeys created by end of 2024. Apple iOS 26 (WWDC25): passkey-only auth (no fallback password) supported as primary in iOS apps. Chime 2023: biometric-gate adoption 88% within first week (mobile fintech). Apple Pay biometric flow: 99%+ user adoption among Apple Pay enabled accounts. WhatsApp / Signal (E2E messaging): biometric lock optional, ~30% adoption (privacy-conscious users). Banking apps biometric adoption: 80-90% within first month (Chase, Wells Fargo, Chime, Cash App). RevenueCat State of Subs 2025: biometric-gate adoption correlates with 18% higher D30 retention in fintech category (cohort comparison).
measurable_outcome_metric: Biometric enrollment rate (% who enroll Face ID / Touch ID / fingerprint within first session — target ≥85% in fintech, ≥60% in consumer). Passkey enrollment rate (% who enroll passkey within D7 — target ≥40% in 2025-2026). Repeat-session auth-time (target ≤2 seconds vs 30-60s magic-link). Auth abandonment rate at biometric prompt (target ≤2%). Biometric fallback rate (% who fall back to passcode when biometric fails — target ≤5%). D7 retention with biometric vs without (cleavage metric). Phishing-attempt success rate (target 0% — passkeys are phishing-resistant by design).
conversion_lift_benchmark: Google: 4× faster sign-in vs password. 1Password: 86% passkey-trial-retention. Stytch: 40-60% passkey adoption in 6 months. Chime biometric-gate: 88% adoption week-one. RevenueCat 2025 fintech: +18% D30 retention with biometric.
engineering_tactics: ARCHITECTURE: Use BIOMETRIC GATE for repeat-session unlock; PASSKEYS for primary auth (replacing magic link OR password). (1) BIOMETRIC GATE FLOW: User auths first time via magic-link / Sign in with Apple. App requests biometric enrollment (LAContext on iOS, BiometricPrompt on Android). On subsequent app launches: prompt biometric → unlock session. Fallback: device passcode (built-in OS fallback). (2) PASSKEY FLOW: First-time user — auth via magic link → app prompts 'Set up passkey for faster sign-in?' → ASAuthorizationController.performRequests with ASAuthorizationPasskeyRegistrationRequest (iOS) / Credential Manager createCredential (Android). Server registers public key. Repeat sign-in: ASAuthorizationPasskeyAssertionRequest → biometric prompt → cryptographic assertion → server verifies → session. (3) SECURE STORAGE: PRIVATE KEY MUST STAY DEVICE-LOCAL — Secure Enclave (iOS) or Trusted Execution Environment / StrongBox (Android). NEVER store biometric data server-side (compliance violation — biometric is special category data under GDPR Article 9; CA CCPA, IL BIPA, TX CUBI). (4) iOS IMPLEMENTATION: import LocalAuthentication, AuthenticationServices. LAContext().evaluatePolicy(.deviceOwnerAuthenticationWithBiometrics) for biometric. ASAuthorizationController + ASAuthorizationPlatformPublicKeyCredentialProvider for passkey. associated-domains entitlement for webcredentials. (5) ANDROID IMPLEMENTATION: BiometricPrompt API + BiometricManager for biometric. CredentialManager.createCredential / getCredential with CreatePublicKeyCredentialRequest / GetPublicKeyCredentialOption for passkey. assetlinks.json for App Links domain. (6) FALLBACK SEQUENCE: biometric → device passcode → magic-link sign-in. (7) PASSKEY CROSS-DEVICE: passkey synced via iCloud Keychain (Apple devices) / Google Password Manager (Android) / 1Password / Dashlane (cross-platform). User can sign in on new device by authenticating with their cloud-synced passkey. Or scan QR code from existing device (FIDO2 hybrid transports). (8) SERVER: WebAuthn library (e.g., @simplewebauthn/server, Stytch SDK, Clerk Passkeys) verifies signed assertions. Store public key + credentialId per user. (9) RECOVERY: if user loses device + cloud-sync, fall back to magic-link via verified email (recovery via possession-of-email, downgrade auth assurance). (10) COPPA-FRIENDLY: under-13 family apps — no password reset complexity for kids; biometric/passkey-only auth simplifies parental flow. (11) LIVENESS / ANTI-SPOOF: iOS Face ID + Touch ID have built-in liveness detection. Android biometrics class 3 (BIOMETRIC_STRONG) only — class 2 (BIOMETRIC_WEAK) like face-only Android phones less secure.
real_world_examples: (1) Apple Pay (foundational consumer biometric): Face ID / Touch ID for every transaction; 99%+ user adoption. (2) Chase / Wells Fargo / Bank of America mobile: biometric-gate on every app launch; 80-90% adoption. (3) Cash App: biometric-gate enrollment immediately post-onboarding; protects $100B+ annual transaction volume. (4) Chime: 88% biometric adoption week-one. (5) Robinhood: biometric-gate optional but strongly recommended. (6) 1Password / Bitwarden / Dashlane: passkey storage + biometric vault unlock. (7) Google account: passkey support 2022+; passwordless option for Google account in 2024+. (8) Apple ID: passkey support iOS 16+; passwordless Apple ID 2024+. (9) Microsoft account: passkey support 2023+. (10) PayPal: passkey for repeat checkout. (11) Amazon: passkey for sign-in 2023+. (12) eBay: passkey support. (13) Best Buy: passkey support. (14) Shopify checkout: passkey support 2024. (15) Stripe / Square: biometric for merchant app. (16) WhatsApp / Signal / Telegram: biometric chat-lock. (17) Note app for sensitive content: biometric-lock. (18) Health apps: HealthKit biometric-gate for sensitive data access. (19) Cal AI biometric-gate for repeat sessions (optional). (20) Mau Baron Prayer Lock biometric-gate (optional, paired with magic link).
mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile-primary for biometric (camera + fingerprint sensor + Secure Enclave). Web equivalent: WebAuthn passkeys via cloud-synced credentials (iCloud Keychain accessible from Safari on macOS; Google Password Manager from Chrome). Cross-device: passkey synced via cloud (Apple iCloud Keychain, Google Password Manager) OR FIDO2 hybrid transport (QR code from desktop browser → phone biometric → desktop session). Web browsers: Safari (iCloud Keychain), Chrome (Google Password Manager), Firefox (3rd-party password manager only — limited), Edge (Microsoft + 3rd-party). Apple Sign in with Apple via web supports passkey; Google sign-in via web supports passkey since 2023. Web-specific: less universal than mobile because OS-level biometric integration on desktop is browser-dependent (Touch ID on Mac works in Safari).
platform_design_language_alignment: Biometric prompt UI is OS-controlled — cannot customize (iOS Face ID animation, Android BiometricPrompt sheet). App can customize the lead-up screen ('Set up Face ID' explainer). Liquid Glass alert sheets (iOS 26+) work for the explainer screen. M3E supports BiometricPrompt-equivalent expressive bottom sheet. Apple HIG mandates: don't show OS biometric prompt without context (prepare user with branded screen first). Apple Sign in with Apple is platform-native passkey/biometric flow combined.
category_fit: STRONGEST: fintech (item 56, near-universal in 2025-2026), enterprise SaaS (workforce auth via WorkOS / Okta + passkeys), healthcare (HIPAA assurance), password managers (1Password, Bitwarden), high-security messaging (Signal, WhatsApp). UNIVERSAL FIT: any consumer app with sensitive data (notes, journal, photos) — biometric-lock optional. EXCELLENT FOR: COPPA-compliant family apps (under-13 biometric-only auth simplifies parental flow). MODERATE: AI-wrapper / chat-first (item 57) — passkey adoption ~30-40% as of 2025. PRODUCTIVITY (item 55) — passkey + SSO is the 2025-2026 enterprise default. WELLNESS (item 54) — biometric optional; not load-bearing.
solo_dev_feasibility: 1_week for biometric-gate (LAContext / BiometricPrompt — straightforward APIs). 1_week to 1_month for passkey (more complex — WebAuthn server + client). Use managed: Stytch Passkeys, Clerk Passkeys, Supabase Auth (passkey support 2024+) — same-day. Self-host: SimpleWebAuthn library (@simplewebauthn/server + @simplewebauthn/browser) is the canonical open-source approach. iOS native: AuthenticationServices framework documentation. Android native: Credential Manager API. Critical: Secure Enclave / TEE storage (don't roll custom — use platform APIs). Test on physical devices (simulators have limited biometric support).
tooling_options: Managed (passkey + biometric): Stytch Passkeys (best DX), Clerk Passkeys, Supabase Auth (passkey 2024+), Auth0 Passkeys, Magic.link Passkeys, Descope, Hanko (open-source). Self-host: SimpleWebAuthn (open-source, dominant). iOS native: AuthenticationServices framework, LocalAuthentication framework, ASAuthorizationController + ASAuthorizationPlatformPublicKeyCredentialProvider. Android native: Credential Manager API (androidx.credentials), BiometricPrompt API (androidx.biometric). Apple Sign in with Apple — bundles passkey + biometric. Google Identity Services — bundles passkey via Credential Manager. Microsoft Entra ID — passkey for enterprise. 1Password / Bitwarden / Dashlane — passkey storage. WebAuthn libraries: @simplewebauthn (TS), webauthn4j (Java), python-fido2, py_webauthn.
ai_dependency: none. Biometric / passkey is cryptographic + biological — no AI involved.
claude_design_application: Prompt fragment: 'Build biometric gate + passkey auth. (1) BIOMETRIC GATE for repeat-session unlock: After first-auth via magic-link (item 60), prompt user to enroll Face ID / Touch ID / fingerprint via LAContext (iOS) / BiometricPrompt (Android). On subsequent app launches: gate access behind biometric prompt. Fallback: device passcode. (2) PASSKEY for primary auth: After first-auth via magic-link, prompt "Set up passkey for faster sign-in?" → ASAuthorizationController.performRequests with ASAuthorizationPlatformPublicKeyCredentialRegistrationRequest (iOS) / CredentialManager.createCredential with CreatePublicKeyCredentialRequest (Android). Server-side: WebAuthn registration via @simplewebauthn/server or Stytch / Clerk passkey API. (3) PRIVATE KEY STAYS DEVICE-LOCAL — Secure Enclave (iOS) / TEE / StrongBox (Android). NEVER store biometric data server-side (GDPR Article 9 + IL BIPA + TX CUBI violation). (4) FALLBACK SEQUENCE: biometric → device passcode → magic-link recovery. (5) iOS associated-domains entitlement for webcredentials. Android assetlinks.json for App Links. (6) Recovery flow: lost device + lost cloud-sync = magic-link recovery to verified email (downgrade auth assurance). (7) COPPA-friendly for under-13 family apps — biometric/passkey-only auth simplifies parental flow. (8) Telemetry: biometric_enrolled, biometric_authenticated, biometric_failed, passkey_registered, passkey_authenticated, passkey_revoked. Forbid: storing biometric data server-side, password-required fallback as default (use device passcode), missing recovery flow.' Second fragment: 'Build the biometric-enrollment screen — single-screen explainer with Sage register: "Use Face ID to sign in faster". Subhead: "Your face stays on your device — never sent to our servers." Primary CTA "Use Face ID" triggers LAContext.evaluatePolicy. Secondary text-link "Skip for now". NO ✨ emoji on biometric explanation, NO indigo-500 gradient on primary button. Use solid brand color and the iOS/Android system biometric glyph (NOT decorative).'
stitch_application: Prompt fragment: 'Mockup the biometric enrollment screen. Single-screen explainer. Top 30%: large platform-system Face ID glyph (iOS Face ID symbol) or fingerprint glyph (Android), monochrome ink #1A1816, NOT colored gradient, NOT decorative emoji. Headline 32pt Cabinet Grotesk weight 400 "Sign in with Face ID". Subhead 16pt Söhne neutral-grey "Faster and more secure than a password. Your face stays on your device — never sent to our servers." Below: 3-bullet vertical-list (NOT three-column grid) with custom number marks 01., 02., 03.: "01. Faster sign-in", "02. More secure", "03. Privacy preserved" — Söhne 14pt. Bottom: primary CTA "Use Face ID" 56pt height, solid ember-orange #E36B2C (NOT gradient), ink-color text. Below: secondary text-link "Skip for now" centered, 14pt Söhne ink/60. Background: solid bone #F4EFE6, NOT gradient. Forbid: Lucide Fingerprint+Lock+Shield icon trinity (use platform-system glyph), purple-blue gradient, ✨🔒🛡️ emoji decorations, Inter font, hover:scale-105, three-column feature grid, indigo-500 button, rounded-xl on every element. Aesthetic: financial-app-restraint — Sage register, monochrome, single accent.'
ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI typically (1) skips biometric / passkey enrollment entirely (defaults to password-only), (2) defaults to indigo-500 + purple-blue gradient on biometric explainer screen (inappropriate for security-context), (3) uses Lucide Fingerprint + Lock + Shield icon trinity instead of platform-system biometric glyph (visual clash with native dialog that follows), (4) writes overly-marketing 'Unlock with Face ID ✨' copy (Sage register, NOT Magician), (5) skips Secure Enclave / TEE storage instructions (security violation), (6) skips recovery flow (lost device = lost account permanently), (7) tries to capture biometric data server-side (cardinal violation — GDPR Article 9, IL BIPA $100M+ class actions: Snapchat, Six Flags), (8) skips iOS associated-domains entitlement / Android assetlinks.json (passkey domain binding broken). Counter-prompt: 'Build biometric-gate + passkey auth with EXPLICIT REQUIREMENTS: (1) Use LAContext (iOS) or BiometricPrompt (Android) — DO NOT roll custom face detection. (2) Use ASAuthorizationController (iOS) or Credential Manager (Android) for passkey. (3) PRIVATE KEY STAYS DEVICE-LOCAL — Secure Enclave / TEE only. NEVER store biometric data server-side (BIPA $100M+ class action precedent). (4) Sage register copy on enrollment screen, NOT Magician. (5) Use platform-system biometric glyph, NOT Lucide icon trinity. (6) Recovery flow via magic-link to verified email. (7) iOS associated-domains entitlement; Android assetlinks.json. (8) Forbid: indigo-500 button, purple-blue gradient, ✨🔒🛡️ emoji, Inter font, three-column "Why biometric" feature grid.'
anti_patterns: (1) Storing biometric data server-side (GDPR Article 9 violation; IL BIPA $100M+ class actions — Snapchat, Six Flags, Facebook). (2) Custom face-detection (rolling own biometric — security catastrophe; use platform APIs). (3) Skipping passkey + biometric in 2025-2026 fintech / enterprise (table-stakes). (4) Indigo-500 + gradient + Lucide icon trinity on biometric explainer (slop). (5) Magician register on biometric copy ('Unlock the magic ✨'). (6) Missing recovery flow (lost device = lost account). (7) Skipping Secure Enclave / TEE (security violation). (8) Skipping passkey cross-device sync setup (iCloud Keychain / Google Password Manager) — single-device passkey is fragile. (9) Auto-enrolling biometric without consent screen (UX violation + GDPR). (10) Binding biometric to specific device only (no fallback for device replacement). (11) Web-version skipping WebAuthn entirely (out of doctrine in 2024-2026). (12) Passkey not phishing-resistant due to misconfigured domain (defeats security purpose).
regulatory_risk: High if mishandled, low if properly implemented. (a) GDPR Article 9 — biometric data is special category; explicit consent + purpose limitation. (b) Illinois Biometric Information Privacy Act (BIPA) — strict consent + retention limits; class actions ($100M+ Snapchat, $650M Facebook, $36M Six Flags). (c) Texas CUBI (Capture or Use of Biometric Identifier). (d) California CCPA / CPRA — biometric is sensitive personal information. (e) HIPAA — biometric for health auth requires BAA. (f) NIST 800-63B — biometric alone insufficient (combine with possession). (g) FIDO Alliance certification recommended (FIDO2 conformance). (h) PSD2 SCA in EU — biometric satisfies inherence factor. (i) PCI DSS for fintech — biometric acceptable for cardholder auth. (j) COPPA — biometric for under-13 requires VPC. (k) EU eIDAS 2.0 (2026) — digital identity wallet integration for cross-border auth.
platform_constraints_2026: Apple App Store Guideline 5.1.1 — biometric data must stay on-device. Apple Guideline 4.8 — Sign in with Apple required if other social offered. iOS LAContext + ASAuthorizationController + AuthenticationServices framework. iOS associated-domains entitlement for webcredentials (passkey domain binding). Android Credential Manager API (API 34+, recommended) replaces deprecated FidoCredentialManager. Android BiometricPrompt API (API 28+). assetlinks.json for App Links + passkey. Apple Pay / Google Pay biometric flow has separate compliance overlay. Apple iOS 26 (WWDC25) supports passkey-only auth (no fallback password). Google Credential Manager (Android 14+) consolidates passwordless flows. Microsoft Entra ID supports cross-device passkey for enterprise.
accessibility_implications: WCAG 2.2 — biometric prompt OS-controlled; alternative auth path (passcode, magic link) MUST be available for users who can't use biometric (no face recognition for blind users with covered eyes; no fingerprint for users with cuts/burns). Sufficient time (SC 2.2.1) — biometric retry should not lock account quickly. Color-not-only-conveyed (SC 1.4.1) — biometric prompt must show text not just icon. Focus (SC 2.4.7) — fallback button must be keyboard-accessible. Touch target 24×24 px (SC 2.5.8) — fallback button. Voice control (iOS Voice Control, Android Voice Access) — should support biometric trigger. Support for users without biometric capability — ALWAYS provide passcode + magic-link fallback. EU EAA 2025 enforcement.
pitfalls_caveats: (1) Biometric data SERVER-SIDE STORAGE = catastrophic compliance violation. Stay device-local. (2) Custom face/fingerprint detection = security catastrophe; use platform APIs only. (3) Passkey domain binding misconfig (associated-domains / assetlinks.json) = passkey doesn't work. (4) Cross-device sync requires user iCloud Keychain / Google Password Manager active — disabled accounts = single-device passkey fragility. (5) Recovery flow critical — lost device + lost cloud-sync = locked out. Always provide magic-link recovery. (6) Anti-spoof: iOS Face ID + Touch ID built-in; Android requires class 3 (BIOMETRIC_STRONG) — class 2 face-only often spoofable. (7) iOS Face ID with mask requires iOS 15.4+. (8) Biometric enrollment user-flow: AVOID blocking onboarding behind biometric (allow skip-for-now); some users won't enroll until trust built. (9) GDPR Article 9 + BIPA + TX CUBI = explicit consent screen required (not buried in privacy policy). (10) WebAuthn cross-platform interop varies by browser; test on Safari + Chrome + Firefox + Edge. (11) Passkey deletion / revocation flow user-accessible. (12) Family / shared-device scenarios — passkey is per-user-account; family devices need account-switching consideration.
cross_cultural_validity: JP: biometric adoption ~75% (high — privacy-conscious + security-conscious). KR: ~80% biometric adoption. CN: domestic biometric ecosystem (Alipay, WeChat Pay all biometric-default); foreign apps face firewall to FIDO Alliance servers. EU: GDPR Article 9 strict consent; PSD2 SCA biometric acceptable for inherence factor. UK: similar to EU + UK FCA guidance. LATAM: biometric adoption growing; mobile-fintech leaders (Nubank, Mercado Pago) standardize on biometric. India: Aadhaar biometric national identity overlay; IndiaStack APIs allow Aadhaar-bridge auth. MENA: biometric adoption high; UAE Pass + Saudi Absher national identity bridges. RTL Arabic: passkey UI translates; biometric prompt OS-controlled.
prescribed_action: DEFAULT for fintech (item 56) — biometric-gate enrollment immediately after first-auth (magic link or KYC), gate every app launch. RECOMMENDED for all consumer apps (items 54, 55, 57) — biometric-gate optional but strongly recommended. PASSKEYS as primary auth replacing magic link (item 60) for security-sensitive verticals — 2025-2026 transition window with magic-link fallback. SEQUENCE: first-time user → magic link → biometric-enrollment screen ('Use Face ID to sign in faster — your face stays on your device') → ASAuthorizationController for passkey enrollment → repeat sessions use biometric / passkey. PRIVATE KEY STAYS DEVICE-LOCAL (Secure Enclave / TEE). NEVER store biometric data server-side (BIPA $100M+ class actions). RECOVERY via magic link to verified email if device + cloud-sync lost. Use platform APIs (LAContext, BiometricPrompt, ASAuthorizationController, Credential Manager). Use managed for solo-dev (Stytch Passkeys, Clerk Passkeys, Supabase Auth passkey 2024+). Sage register copy ('Sign in with Face ID — faster and more secure'), NOT Magician ('Unlock the magic ✨'). Forbid: indigo-500 + purple-blue gradient on biometric explainer, Lucide icon trinity (use platform-system glyph), Magician register copy, server-side biometric storage, missing recovery flow.
questionnaire_relevance: N/A — auth is upstream of questionnaire. Biometric-enrollment screen is a separate single-screen flow post-first-auth, before main onboarding starts.
aha_relevance: Biometric / passkey is NOT the aha — but biometric-gate makes repeat-session entry frictionless, preserving the daily aha cycle (Headspace user opens app, Face ID glance, immediately into meditation).
telemetry_events: biometric_enrollment_screen_viewed, biometric_enrolled, biometric_skipped, biometric_authenticated, biometric_failed, biometric_fallback_to_passcode, biometric_revoked, passkey_enrollment_screen_viewed, passkey_registered, passkey_skipped, passkey_authenticated, passkey_revoked, passkey_recovery_invoked. Cohort: D7 retention by enrollment status.
measurement_horizon: Day 0 (enrollment); Day 7 (repeat-session frequency); Day 30 (D30 retention by enrollment status).
emotional_archetype_alignment: PRIMARY: Sage (security wisdom — 'faster and more secure'), Ruler (institutional authority — 'bank-grade security'). MISMATCHES: Magician ('unlock the magic ✨' — undermines security gravitas), Outlaw (rebellion clashes with security context), Jester (humor undermines security trust), overly-Caregiver (gentle pacing reads weak in security context). Neutral acceptable: Innocent (purity of biometric-as-natural).
anti_pattern_paired: Pairs against Premature Authentication (combined with magic link, item 60, to remove the antipattern). Pairs against Server-Side Biometric Storage, Custom-Rolled Biometric Detection, Missing Recovery Flow, Indigo-500-Slop on Security Screens, Magician Register on Biometric Copy.
